threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the 106 bus is the way to get around instead of mastering the train lines. It's a rookie mistake, mate.
Caught a Night Bus 133 the other night. A guy debated loudly with his empty can of cider about existentialism. Brixton’s late-night charm is unmatched.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw the guy in a full suit, arguing passionately with a pigeon over a piece of bread near the Maybachufer. That's Neukölln for you.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a late-night currywurst stand that also doubles as a gallery. Art hangs on the walls, but the ketchup is still questionable.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their eye roll when tourists block the sidewalk taking selfies in front of the same street art for the hundredth time.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a whiff of fresh pastries from a nearby bakery while stuck at Civic Center. Dog on the tracks? More like a cafe break for the drivers.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all of Gràcia is about fancy bars—most locals still grab their morning café at the same corner spot for decades, far from the crowds.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You'll see them struggling with the metro map, often staring at it like it's a treasure map, while locals navigate effortlessly.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the Overground is a reliable option for getting anywhere on time. Bless your heart, you’ll learn soon enough.
Caught the N35 last weekend; a guy with a saxophone serenading everyone, but it was the owl outside that stole the show. Perfect harmony with the night.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I saw a wild rabbit munching on leftover döner in the park while a kid practiced breakdancing nearby. Classic Neukölln chaos.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you find a bar that serves cocktails in real fruit skins—think coconut shells for piña coladas. It’s a wild flavor and aesthetic twist.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local when they still call it 'Civic Center' and don’t flinch at seeing a dog lounging on the Muni tracks like it owns the place.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the Muni today, spotted a guy in full business attire, backpack, and slippers. Only in the Tenderloin can you mix hustle with laid-back vibes.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think every corner in Gràcia has a bar with cheap cervezas, but it's the hidden plazas and local bodegas that keep the true vibe alive.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them by their fascination with the street performers in Plaça del Sol—they’re the ones taking endless photos instead of just enjoying the show.
